title: Woman developed acute kidney injury after replacing meals with vegetable juices for weight loss

title: Born in the USA: Supreme Court rebuffs Trump's bid to end birthright citizenship

This ruling removes a key immigration initiative from the administration's agenda.

description: Europe, once crafted to stay warm during harsh winters, now grapples with a grim new challenge: relentless heatwaves. title: Remember endless tabs & rabbit holes? How AI is changing the web we grew up with
description: The internet's evolution from a browsing landscape to an AI-driven answer engine is reshaping user habits. Once a journey of exploration, the web now offers instant, customized responses, saving time but potentially narrowing discovery. This shift impacts digital publishing and marketing, forcing a redefinition of online visibility beyond traditional clicks. While offering convenience, AI browsing also raises significant privacy and security concerns.

‘Is she having an affair?’ Ketan's father claims in FIR his son suspected Siya's link with Chetan
description: A Pune realtor's tragic fall from Lohagad Fort has led to the arrest of his fiancée and her alleged paramour, believed to be involved in his death. Reports indicate that the victim had voiced concerns regarding infidelity and was troubled by his fiancée's actions before the fatal incident. Ignoring family warnings about her history due to their connections, the investigation now delves deeper into this suspected murder case.

title: Over 10,000 Etihad Rail tickets sold as UAE launches first passenger train service: Routes, travel time, fares
'Huge distraction': Strauss questions timing of Ben Stokes' retirement
description: Former England captain Andrew Strauss has voiced concerns over Ben Stokes' retirement announcement during the crucial Test against New Zealand, deeming it a significant distraction. Strauss believes such news should be shared either before or after a match to maintain team focus. Stokes, an exceptional all-rounder, retires with numerous records, including the most Test sixes and a rare double of over 7,000 runs and 250 wickets.

title: Google makes Gemini’s personalised image generation free for eligible users in US
‘I tried to talk him down’: McCullum on Ben Stokes’ sudden retirement
Canada says ‘best time ever’ for Indian students, but numbers tell a different story
Nine years of GST: One tax, one market, many transformations
description: India's Goods and Services Tax (GST), rolled out in 2017, has transformed indirect taxation, creating a unified national market and boosting formalisation. Collections have surged, and businesses now operate digitally, improving efficiency. Consumers benefit from reduced tax cascading and greater price transparency. The next phase, GST 2.0, focuses on simplification, technology, and dispute resolution to further enhance ease of doing business and economic growth.

title: 'I'm very happy': Jadeja reacts to Ben Stokes' shock retirement
description: Former India captain Ajay Jadeja lauded Ben Stokes' retirement, calling it the "right call" and a testament to his refusal to be dictated by external forces. Jadeja admired Stokes' principled stand, suggesting the all-rounder wouldn't tolerate administrators controlling his personal life.  Meanwhile, Sanjay Manjrekar expressed sadness, calling Stokes' departure an "end of an era" and highlighting his match-winning prowess on the big stage.

title: ‘Could be a disaster’: Ankur Warikoo on new WhatsApp username feature
CBI arrests 2 former bank officials in Rs 504-crore IDFC First fraud
Mumbai rains: 11-year-old dies as tree crushes school van carrying 15 students
description: The incident took place near Heritage Pride and was reported to the Mumbai Fire Brigade at 2.58 pm. Fire brigade personnel, police, a 108 ambulance and BMC ward staff rushed to the spot and carried out a rescue operation. Officials said all 15 students were travelling in the Universal High School van when the tree was uprooted and fell on the vehicle.

'Nothing to do with India': England cricketer clarifies after accusing ICC of bias
description: England cricketers Kate Cross and Alex Hartley have faced severe backlash, including death threats, after their criticism of the ICC's Women's T20 World Cup semi-final scheduling was misinterpreted. They clarified their comments targeted the tournament's policy, not the Indian team, which was automatically placed in an earlier slot if they qualified. The controversy became moot when India was eliminated, reverting the schedule to the standard format.

Psychology says people who love jungle safaris are often high in ‘Openness to Experience’
description: Thrill-seekers drawn to jungle safaris often exhibit high 'Openness to Experience,' a personality trait linked to curiosity and a desire for novel encounters. Psychologists suggest this trait fuels the enjoyment of unpredictable wildlife sightings and the immersive journey itself. Beyond excitement, nature's embrace is found to enhance creative thinking and foster a deeper connection to the environment, inspiring conservation efforts.

title: Weather tomorrow: IMD issues thunderstorms and gusty winds; heavy rain warning for several regions
description: Widespread rain, thunderstorms, and gusty winds are forecast across India as the southwest monsoon remains active. Heavy to very heavy rainfall is expected in Konkan & Goa and Madhya Maharashtra. Northern, central, and eastern states will see thunderstorms with lightning and strong winds. Delhi anticipates moderate rain and thunderstorms, with temperatures set to drop.

title: '994 days of genocide': Microsoft engineer quits, emails thousands on his final day
description: A Microsoft data center technician in Italy resigned on his final day, emailing thousands of colleagues across Europe to accuse the company of powering Israel's war in Gaza through its Azure cloud and European data centers. Published by No Azure for Apartheid, the email cites the Unit 8200 surveillance exposé and urges Microsoft workers to "reclaim our labor" and divest.

title: Cowboys linked to $53M Colts star in blockbuster NFL trade proposal
description: The Dallas Cowboys have been named a potential landing spot for Indianapolis Colts star Jonathan Taylor in a proposed trade. CBS Sports' Garrett Podell suggested the All-Pro running back could be available for a third-round pick if the Colts struggle in 2026. With Taylor entering the final year of his contract and coming off another standout season, Dallas could pursue him if its rushing attack fails to meet expectations.

title: Brittany Mahomes shares heartfelt promise inspired by Patrick Mahomes
description: Brittany Mahomes says becoming a mother has made her more ambitious and intentional about pursuing her goals. She hopes her children, Sterling, Bronze, and Golden, see both her and Patrick Mahomes working hard and following their dreams. Brittany believes leading by example will teach them that success comes through determination and perseverance. She also shared that her daughter Sterling has shown growing interest in her work, making those moments even more meaningful.

title: Michael Dell gives update on Dell's plan to move company's base to Texas
description: Dell Technologies is officially moving its legal base from Delaware to Texas, with 97% shareholder approval. CEO Michael Dell expressed pride in returning the company to its founding state, highlighting Texas's role in its growth. This move follows a trend of major corporations relocating to Texas, attracted by its business-friendly climate.

title: Who are Erling Haaland's parents? Inside the Norway star's sporting family
description: Erling Haaland comes from one of Norway’s most accomplished sporting families. His father, former Premier League player Alf-Inge Haaland, and his mother, former Norwegian heptathlon champion Gry Marita Braut, both had elite athletic careers. The Manchester City striker is also close to his siblings Astor and Gabrielle Haaland, has two younger half-sisters, and has been in a long-term relationship with Isabel Haugseng Johansen, with whom he shares a son born in 2024.
